page: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Daldv6 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 64 dedicated vCPUs, which correspond to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance includes 3780 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which accelerate vector and neural network operations. Cryptographic tasks are accelerated via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets, while integer operations benefit from BMI2, ADX, and FMA flags. Virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V with SVM support.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64alds_v6 demonstrates strong compute and memory capabilities. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is in the top 10% with a score of 3079.97, and multi-core performance is in the top 25% at 98458.65. Geekbench multi-core testing places the instance in the top 10% with a score of 22158.0. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong at 4863.19 GB/sec, though large uncached block read bandwidth drops to an average tier of 151.26 GB/sec. Large block write bandwidth remains strong at 103.51 GB/sec. The instance also shows strong performance in database operations, achieving 88.46 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a PassMark database score of 29533.79.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D64alds_v6 is a low-memory configuration relative to its high compute density. This represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications but provides high cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. The combination of 64 dedicated vCPUs and 3780 GB of local storage makes this instance highly suitable for software compilation, static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations. It is also capable of handling small-scale LLM inference, achieving 3962.73 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M model, though performance scales down to 3.5 tokens per second for text generation on a 70B model.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64alds_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Daldv6 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores), 128.0 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and SHA_NI. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside strong database and memory write capabilities. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads. It is well-suited for software compilation, high-throughput static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations that benefit from fast local storage but do not require massive memory footprints.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC CPU.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, fast local storage, and advanced vector acceleration for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz
128 GB system memory and 3780 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Advanced vector and cryptographic instruction support (AVX-512, AES)
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and database tasks
